title: JSON node storage via pmm AVL-tree forest
description: JSON nodes must be allocated, stored, and managed exclusively through pmm's AVL-tree forest mechanism. Each JSON value (null, boolean, integer, real, string, array, object) is represented as a pmm-managed block. Arrays use parray for O(1) index access. Objects use pmap for O(log n) key lookup. Strings use pstringview (interned, read-only) for keys and pstring (mutable) for values.
rationale: This eliminates the separate pool allocator used in the BinDiffSynchronizer prototype and aligns with BR-002's unified architecture requirement.
author: netkeep80
acceptance_criteria: JSON nodes use pmm allocation (no ppool or custom pool)
Arrays backed by parray with O(1) index access
Objects backed by pmap with O(log n) key lookup
String keys use pstringview for interning and deduplication
String values use pstring for mutability
